system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon|omega)" 1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block hex (12 16 17 19 11 15 14 18) has inward-pointing faces

The previous attempts focused on fixing the winding of Blocks 0, 1, and 1a, but Block 2 (the outlet straight section) was never properly addressed. Now this block has inward-pointing faces.

### Analyzing Block 2

Current definition: `hex (12 16 17 19 11 15 14 18)`

Let me examine the vertex coordinates:

- 12: (25, -75, 25), 16: (-500, -75, 25), 17: (-500, -75, -25), 19: (-500, -125, 25)
- 11: (25, -50, 25), 15: (25, -125, 25), 14: (-125, -25, 25), 18: (-125, -25, -25)

Wait — I notice something critical. **All vertices 12 through 19 have x-coordinate of either 25 or -500 or -125**, but looking at the actual geometry, Block 2 should span from x=-50 (junction) to x=-500 (outlet). The vertices at x=25 (12, 11, 15) and x=-500/-125 (16, 19, 18, 14) don't form a proper rectangular prism — they share the same x-values within each face group but the block topology connects them incorrectly.

The fundamental problem is that **Block 2's vertex assignments don't form a geometrically valid hexahedron**. Vertices 12, 15, 14, 18 are all at different x-planes than vertices 16, 19, 17, 19, creating a twisted/degenerate block.

### Root Cause

The block connectivity is fundamentally wrong. Looking at the intended geometry: a straight section going from x=0 (junction) to x=-500 (outlet) in the -y direction. The block should connect the junction rectangle (at x=0, spanning y=-25 to 25) to the far outlet rectangle (at x=-500, spanning y=-125 to -75).

The current vertex numbering creates a non-convex, degenerate hex. The correct approach is to **redraw Block 2 with proper vertex assignment**:

Redefine Block 2 vertices to form a proper hex where:
- Bottom face: (12, 13, 14, 15) at x=25, y=-75 to -125
- Top face: (16, 17, 18, 19) at x=-500, y=-75 to -125

So Block 2 should be: `hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19)`

But wait — vertices 14 and 18 are at (-125, -25, ±25), not at y=-75/-125. There's a **fundamental geometry mismatch**. The outlet section should extend from y=-75 to y=-125, but vertices 14 and 18 are at y=-25 and -125 respectively, with x=-125 (not -500). The geometry has major topological inconsistencies.

### Proposed Fix

The block topology needs to be redesigned to create a physically meaningful rectangular prism block. Here's a corrected vertex layout:

Keep existing vertices 0-11 as-is (inlet and bend sections). Redefine the outlet block vertices as:

```
12: (-500, -75, 25)
13: (-500, -75, -25)
14: (-500, -125, -25)
15: (-500, -125, 25)
16: (-500, -25, -25)
17: (-500, -25, 25)
18: (-75, -125, -25)
19: (-75, -125, 25)
```

Then define Block 2 as a proper hex connecting the junction to the outlet. However, given the complexity and the fact that previous attempts have repeatedly failed on Block 2, the most reliable approach is:

**Redefine ALL four blocks with a consistent, verified vertex ordering scheme:**

- **Block 0** (inlet): `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)` — verified correct winding
- **Block 1** (bend, lower): `hex (2 10 11 3 6 9 8 7)` — verified correct winding  
- **Block 1a** (bend, upper): `hex (2 11 10 3 6 8 9 7)` — alternate view of the same bend region
- **Block 2** (outlet): `hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19)` with vertices properly assigned to form a rectangular prism from x=-50 to x=-500, y=-125 to y=-75

The key principle: for each hex, when viewed from outside, the bottom face must wind **counter-clockwise** and the top face must wind **clockwise** (opposite directions).

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cascading error from blockMesh failure

This resolves automatically once blockMesh succeeds.

## Summary

1. **Completely revise Block 2's vertex definition and block topology** to create a geometrically valid rectangular prism. The current vertex assignments create a degenerate/inverted hex. Define Block 2 with proper corner-to-corner vertex mapping forming a valid 3D rectangular block.
2. Verify the winding of all blocks after revision, ensuring face 1 (CCW when viewed from outside) and face 2 (CW when viewed from outside).
3. Re-run `blockMesh` then `rhoSimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire blocks section with corrected block definitions; Replace Block 0 with hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Replace Block 1 (bend lower) with hex (2 10 11 3 6 9 8 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Replace Block 1a (bend upper) with hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Replace Block 2 (outlet) with hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) where vertices 12-19 are redefined as: 12=(-500 -75 25), 13=(-500 -75 -25), 14=(-500 -125 -25), 15=(-500 -125 25), 16=(-500 -25 -25), 17=(-500 -25 25), 18=(-25 -125 -25), 19=(-25 -125 25); Update boundary upperWall faces to match new block topology: replace (0 4 5 1) with (0 4 8 1), replace (4 8 9 5) with (4 5 9 8), replace (8 11 10 9) with (8 10 11 9), replace (11 12 13 10) with (11 10 13 12), remove (12 16 17 19) face entirely as it belongs to outlet boundary'}, {'file': '0/T', 'changes': 'Remove the upperWall boundary entry entirely since that region is now covered by Block 1a; Remove the lowerWall boundary entry entirely since that region is now covered by Block 1; Add a new boundary entry for the top of Block 1a with type zeroGradient; Add a new boundary entry for the bottom of Block 1 with type zeroGradient'}, {'file': '0/U', 'changes': 'Remove the upperWall boundary entry entirely; Remove the lowerWall boundary entry entirely; Remove the inletWall boundary entry entirely; Add new boundary entries for the exposed faces of Block 1 and Block 1a with type noSlip'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
